emails.subject: Your Forum Health assessment results — [FIRST_NAME], a few things stood out
emails.preview: What your pattern is pointing to
emails.body:
Hi [FIRST_NAME] — quick note from the care team at Forum Health Madison.

I reviewed your Custom Health Assessment, and a few patterns came up immediately:

* Primary focus: [CORE_ISSUE_1]
* Secondary contributors: [CORE_ISSUE_2], [CORE_ISSUE_3]
* Common symptoms in this pattern: [SYMPTOM_A], [SYMPTOM_B], [SYMPTOM_C]

What most people miss: effective health care is not about managing symptoms until the next problem surfaces. It is about understanding your specific biology, identifying what is driving your pattern, and building a plan that is actually designed for you.

The 3 things that create real momentum for people with [CORE_ISSUE_1]:

1. Identify the actual driver (not just the symptom it produces)
2. Restore what the body is depleted of — hormones, nutrients, or cellular function
3. Measure the response and adjust — until the body can hold the improvement on its own

Forum Health Madison offers personalized, integrative care with real expertise across hormone health, weight optimization, and chronic condition management.

Your next step:
* Option A: See your recommended first steps inside the app → [VIEW_MY_PLAN_LINK]
* Option B: Book a consultation — we will review your assessment and map a plan → [SCHEDULE_LINK]

— The Forum Health Madison Care Team
Forum Health Madison | Madison, WI | forumhealth.com

emails.subject: Why [PRIMARY_SYMPTOM] often resists standard treatment — explained simply
emails.preview: The part of the picture most clinics miss
emails.body:
Hi [FIRST_NAME],

I want to share something that changes how most people think about their health pattern.

Standard medicine divides the body into systems and treats them independently. What integrative and functional medicine recognizes is that symptoms often emerge at the intersection of multiple systems — and treating just one while ignoring the others produces incomplete results.

For someone with a pattern like yours — where [PRIMARY_SYMPTOM] is a central complaint — the downstream effects usually trace back to one or more of the following: hormonal imbalance, metabolic dysfunction, chronic systemic inflammation, or gut health disruption affecting how nutrients are absorbed and how signals are sent throughout the body.

These do not typically show up on a standard panel. They require a different kind of assessment — and a willingness to look at the full picture.

At Forum Health Madison, we run a thorough intake and a functional panel designed to surface what standard testing misses. Then we build a plan that treats the actual source.

That is how symptoms resolve — not just quiet down temporarily.

— The Forum Health Madison Care Team
Forum Health Madison | Madison, WI | forumhealth.com

[Book a Consultation →]([SCHEDULE_LINK])

emails.subject: What changed for a patient with your pattern
emails.body:
Hi [FIRST_NAME],

One final note — a patient story that might resonate.

A woman in her mid-40s came to us after struggling with weight that was resistant to diet and exercise, persistent fatigue, and low mood that had been attributed to 'stress.' She had tried multiple programs and had been on thyroid medication with partial results.

After a full Forum Health intake — which included hormone panel, metabolic testing, and gut health markers — we identified a pattern of suboptimal hormone conversion and chronic low-grade inflammation that had been unaddressed.

We built a personalized care plan around those specific findings and adjusted it over three months as she responded.

Her energy normalized. The weight resistance shifted. She described her overall sense of wellbeing as 'completely different' from when she first came in.

Individual results vary and depend on many personal and health factors. But the consistent principle is: when the actual root driver is identified and addressed precisely, the body responds.

If your assessment suggests a similar pattern, we would be glad to take a closer look with you.

— The Forum Health Madison Care Team
Forum Health Madison | Madison, WI | forumhealth.com

Disclaimer: Individual results vary. This is not medical advice.

[Schedule Your Appointment →]([SCHEDULE_LINK])

gamma_content.digital_health_report:
🔎 DIGITAL SEO HEALTH REPORT — forumhealth.com
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

📊 DOMAIN SNAPSHOT
──────────────────
| Metric               | Value                      |
|----------------------|----------------------------|
| Global Rank          | #109,051                   |
| Keywords Ranked      | 13,415                     |
| Monthly Traffic      | ~17,283 visits             |
| Traffic Value        | $53,419/mo                 |
| Keywords in Top 10   | 13                         |
| SERP Features Traffic| ~0/mo                      |


🔑 THE CORE FINDING: 13,415 KEYWORDS. ONLY 17,283 MONTHLY VISITORS.
──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
13,415 keywords ranked. Only 17,283 monthly visitors.

Position Breakdown (top 50 highest-volume keywords, pos 1–20):
• Pos #1: 2 keywords (top-of-page, ~28% CTR)
• Pos #2-3: 3 keywords (~15% CTR)
• Pos #4-10: 11 keywords (page 1, ~3-8% CTR)  ← ACTION ZONE
• Pos #11-20: 34 keywords (page 2, <1% CTR)
• Pos #21+: ~13,365 keywords (invisible)

Forum Health has built an impressive 13,000+ keyword footprint — the content authority is clearly there. But with only 17,283 monthly visitors and a traffic value of $53,419/mo, these are high-intent medical keywords converting at a fraction of their potential. The 34 keywords locked on page 2 and 13,000+ buried past page 2 represent the real opportunity.

The CTR reality: Positions 1-3 capture 54% of all clicks. Position 4-10 gets 3-8%.
Moving 11 keywords from pos 4-10 to pos 1-3 could 6-8× their traffic contribution.


🤖 AI SEARCH GAP
──────────────────
• 24 of your ranked keywords trigger Google AI Overviews on search results
• Your practice is cited in 0 of those AI-generated answers

→ You're invisible in AI-powered search despite 24 opportunities.
  Practices cited in AI Overviews receive 20-30% more qualified traffic from those searches.


🎯 BIGGEST OPPORTUNITY: CIRS / CHRONIC INFLAMMATORY RESPONSE SYNDROME
──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
This is Forum Health's most concentrated high-volume, low-competition cluster. All keywords rank on page 2 despite being exactly the conditions Forum Health treats.

Total cluster search volume: ~10,300/mo

[pos 16] cirs symptoms — 3,600/mo  KD:19
[pos 16] chronic inflammatory response syndrome — 2,900/mo  KD:19
[pos 18] sot therapy — 1,900/mo  KD:14
[pos 19] dutch testing — 1,900/mo  KD:18
[pos 20] what are early warning signs of thyroid problems — 1,600/mo  KD:36

You treat CIRS patients and offer DUTCH testing and SOT therapy — but your content isn't structured the way patients search. These five keywords share KD scores of 14–19, meaning low competition. You're on page 2 not because of authority gaps but because of content structure gaps.

Opportunity: Moving cluster from pos 16–20 → pos 3 = ~3,100 additional visits/mo


⚡ WITHIN STRIKING DISTANCE (pos 4-10)
──────────────────────────────────────
[pos 9] cleerly — 2,900 searches/mo
[pos 4] power2patient — 2,400 searches/mo
[pos 6] cleerly heart scan — 1,600 searches/mo
[pos 6] hasimoto — 1,300 searches/mo
[pos 8] reactivated epstein barr virus symptoms — 1,000 searches/mo
[pos 4] functional medicine news — 880 searches/mo

→ Highest priority: "cleerly" at pos 9 — 2,900/mo searches.
  Forum Health already offers Cleerly cardiac CT scans — one dedicated page with proper H1/H2 targeting and schema markup could push this to pos 1-3 within 90 days.


🏆 VS YOUR TOP COMPETITOR
──────────────────────────

fullyfunctional.com:
  Keywords: 6,957  |  Traffic: 6,310/mo  |  Value: $5,673/mo
  Traffic efficiency: 0.9 visits per keyword

forumhealth.com:
  Keywords: 13,415  |  Traffic: 17,283/mo  |  Value: $53,419/mo
  Traffic efficiency: 1.3 visits per keyword

Forum Health outperforms its top SEMrush competitor on every metric — 2× the keywords, 2.7× the traffic, and 9.4× the traffic value. The $53,419 traffic value signals your keywords attract high-intent, high-LTV patients that competitors aren't capturing. The play here isn't to chase competitors — it's to convert more of your existing rankings from page 2 to page 1.


🎯 3-PRIORITY ATTACK PLAN
──────────────────────────

1. CIRS CONTENT ARCHITECTURE
   Build 4 targeted content pages around CIRS symptoms, DUTCH testing, SOT therapy, and thyroid warning signs — each optimized for the exact search phrases in the cluster.
   Target: Move cluster from avg pos 18 → pos 5 = ~2,000 additional visits/mo within 90 days

2. CLEERLY CARDIAC SCAN PAGE
   Create a dedicated /cleerly/ or /cleerly-heart-scan/ page with structured content, patient FAQ schema, and local signals. This keyword has KD:20 and you're already at pos 9.
   Target: Pos 1-3 for "cleerly" + "cleerly heart scan" = ~1,200 additional visits/mo within 60 days

3. AI OVERVIEW CITATION STRATEGY
   24 searches that trigger AI Overviews currently cite zero Forum Health content. Add structured Q&A sections and authoritative citations to your top clinical pages to get cited in AI-generated answers.
   Target: Cited in 5+ AI Overviews within 120 days = 20-30% traffic lift on those searches
